creator: Julie Bort
title: (No AI) Uber is the latest to be won over by Amazon’s AI chips
dc:creator: Julie Bort
content: Uber is expanding its AWS contract to run more of its ride-sharing features on Amazon's chips. This is a thumb-of-the nose at Oracle and Google.
contentSnippet: Uber is expanding its AWS contract to run more of its ride-sharing features on Amazon's chips. This is a thumb-of-the nose at Oracle and Google.

creator: Rebecca Szkutak
title: (No AI) Anthropic ups compute deal with Google and Broadcom amid skyrocketing demand
dc:creator: Rebecca Szkutak
content: Anthropic bulked up its compute deal with Google and Broadcom as the company has seen its run-rate revenue surge to $30 billion.
contentSnippet: Anthropic bulked up its compute deal with Google and Broadcom as the company has seen its run-rate revenue surge to $30 billion.

title: (No AI) Suno and major music labels reportedly clash over AI music sharing
author: Emma Roth
content:
![A smiling computer surrounded by music notes.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/01/STK467_AI_MUSIC_CVirginia_B.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

The AI-powered musicmaker Suno is struggling to reach licensing deals with Universal Music Group and Sony Music Entertainment. That's according to [a report from the _Financial Times_](https://www.ft.com/content/b066a226-4871-4669-97a8-f9617cdbf48b?syn-25a6b1a6=1), which says both sides can't agree on whether users should be able to share the AI-generated songs they create.

"Universal wants AI-generated tracks to stay inside apps such as Suno and not spread freely across the internet. Suno, however, wants users to be able to share and distribute those songs more widely," the _Financial Times_ reports. Suno, which lets users create AI-generated music with a text prompt, [became the subject of a massive copyright lawsuit](https://www.theverge.com/2024/6/24/24184710/riaa-ai-lawsuit-suno-udio-copyright-umg-sony-warner) from U …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/908119/suno-sony-universal-music-ai-disagreement)
contentSnippet:
The AI-powered musicmaker Suno is struggling to reach licensing deals with Universal Music Group and Sony Music Entertainment. That's according to a report from the Financial Times, which says both sides can't agree on whether users should be able to share the AI-generated songs they create.
"Universal wants AI-generated tracks to stay inside apps such as Suno and not spread freely across the internet. Suno, however, wants users to be able to share and distribute those songs more widely," the Financial Times reports. Suno, which lets users create AI-generated music with a text prompt, became the subject of a massive copyright lawsuit from U …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: (No AI) Intel will help build Elon Musk’s Terafab AI chip factory
author: Andrew J. Hawkins
content:
![Vector illustration of the Intel logo.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2025/01/acastro_STK094_02.jpeg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

[Elon Musk's Terafab AI chip project](http://v) in Austin, Texas, is gaining a crucial new partner: Intel.

On Tuesday, the American chipmaker announced it was signing on to help design and build the sprawling facility, which would supply AI chips to Musk's two companies, SpaceX ([newly merged with xAI](https://www.theverge.com/transportation/873203/elon-musk-spacex-xai-merge-data-centers-space-tesla-ipo)) and Tesla. Musk needs AI chips to power his plans to build a "robot army" that includes self-driving cars and humanoid robots, as well as for the data centers he plans on launching into space. SpaceX plans on making its [initial public offering](https://www.theverge.com/science/904991/spacex-announces-ipo-but-its-keeping-the-numbers-secret-for-now) later this year.

"Terafab will close the gap between today's chip production and the future's demand - a future …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/transportation/907976/elon-musk-terafab-intel-ai-chip-spacex-tesla)

SpaceX plans on making its initial public offering later this year.
